methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $42M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Drones & Autonomous (found 1 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 5 same-sector peers exist in Drones & Autonomous, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Sunflower Labs is a Series B stage company operating in the Drones & Autonomous sector. The company has raised $41.7M to date. The relevant comp universe consists of eight private companies in the same sector, including Elroy Air, Joby Aviation, Zipline, Nuro, and Archer Aviation; no public comparables exist in this subsector.

The valuation analysis applies a stage-progression methodology, which is applicable given the Series B stage. Using total raised ($42M) as a baseline proxy and applying a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B to subsequent stage transitions yields an implied valuation of $79M. The Peer-Median method was not applicable due to insufficient narrow peer comparability—while five same-sector peers exist, only one competitor meets same-stage filtering criteria, falling short of the minimum three required for reliable median comparison. Including all-sector peers would introduce material distortion given heterogeneity in drone subsectors and business models.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $67M to $91M, with a median point estimate of $79M. This range reflects sensitivity around stage-appropriate step-up assumptions. Key uncertainties include the absence of near-peer valuations at identical stages and the nascent, high-dispersion nature of the Drones & Autonomous sector, where valuations remain driven by capital intensity and perceived market timing rather than established revenue or profitability metrics.
